youtube_items.summary: our oceans sustain life an abundant ocean can feed a billion people a healthy meal every day forever but now they are being filled killed by throwaway plastics the equivalent of one garbage truck of plastic is dumped in the sea every minute 17 point six billion pounds every year plastic is everywhere in our ocean floating on the surface mixing in the saltwater and sitting on the ocean bottom miles and miles deep and once in the ocean it never goes away over hundreds of years it breaks down into small pieces but those pieces even the tiny ones called micro plastics are still plastic sea turtles are choking on them scientists say that over 60% of whale and dolphin species are affected by it it's all plankton the base of the ocean food chain eat it and so do we it's in the water we drink it's

youtube_items.title: [YT] Recycling plastics – Resource efficiency with an optimized... - YouTube
youtube_items.summary: Not every piece of packaging  makes such a spectacular exit. Millions of packages in Germany simply feel  empty at some point, squeezed out and useless. The result is two and a half million  tons of packaging waste each year   that ends up in the yellow bag or the yellow bin. And from there to a sorting plant.  The problem is different kinds of   plastic are difficult to separate from  each other and from other materials. That's why most waste ends up  being used for energy recovery. The empty packaging is burned for  energy in conventional power plants   for example or in the steel or cement industry. At this sorting plant in southern Hessen the  MEILO company plans to greatly increase the   proportion of reus

analysis.problem: Неправилното управление на отпадъците води до загрязнение на океаните и увреждане на биосферата.
analysis.root_cause: Ниските и средни икономики имат слаби инфраструктури за управление на отпадъците, което довежда до разхвърляне на отпадъци в природните водни обекти.
leverage_points: Развитие на инфраструктура за управление на отпадъците в ниските и средни икономики
Разпространение на образователни програми за устойчиво управление на отпадъците

proposed_actions.action: Инвестирани инвестиции в инфраструктурата за събиране и обработка на отпадъците в ниските и средни икономики
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Увеличение на ефективността на управлението на отпадъците с 30% в 10 града в ниските и средни икономики до 2028 г.

proposed_actions.action: Разработване на образователни програми за училища и обществени организации, насочени към устойчиво управление на отпадъците
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Повишаване на осведомеността за устойчиво управление на отпадъците с 50% в 500 училища до 2027 г.

evidence: 0.5% от всички пластмасови отпадъци достигат до океаните (2026 г.)
Ниските и средните икономики често имат слаби инфраструктури за управление на отпадъците, което води до загрязнение на водни обекти
analysis.generalization: Проблемът на управлението на отпадъците е свързан с домейните planet, materials и society, тъй като неправилното управление на отпадъците влияе върху здравословната биосфера, устойчивостта на ресурсите и социалното благополучие.
analysis.summary: Неправилното управление на отпадъците води до загрязнение на океаните и увреждане на биосферата, особено в ниските и средните икономики.

raw_items.title: California city launches state's first fully electric trash and recycling fleet
Project Zero Waste field trip connects students to real-world sustainability efforts
raw_items.summary: Students from Valadez Middle School recently stepped beyond the classroom and into one of Orange County’s largest waste management facilities to learn firsthand how trash, recycling and composting systems operate.

raw_items.title: SWANA Releases New ARF Report on Horizontal Landfill Gas Collection Systems
raw_items.summary: The SWANA Applied Research Foundation (ARF) released a new report titled Horizontal Landfill Gas Collection Systems. This report provides updated guidance to assist solid waste professionals in designing, installing, and operating horizontal landfill gas (LFG) collection systems.

raw_items.title: Penn researchers are turning food scraps into building materials — from pineapple peels to celery stalks
raw_items.summary: At Penn’s DumoLab, researchers work to solve multiple problems: how to keep food waste out of landfills, make healthier building materials and tackle climate change.

raw_items.title: 7 ocean plastic statistics that actually matter in 2026
raw_items.summary: About 0.5% of all plastic waste ends up in the ocean.[2]. Why it matters: The older, higher figure is still widely repeated, but the distinction matters for solutions. Knowing that rivers are the primary delivery mechanism helps target interventions.

raw_items.title: Impact of improper waste disposal on urban flooding and...
raw_items.summary: Improper solid waste disposal is a pervasive environmental and public-health challenge in many Nigerian cities, contributing directly to increased urban flooding, degradation of water resources, and long-term threats to environmental sustainability.

raw_items.title: Environmental Impact Of Improper Waste Management In Nigeria
raw_items.summary: Common Forms of Improper Waste Disposal in Nigeria. Here are the most widespread improper disposal practices across Nigerian states: Open dumping: Waste piled on vacant lots, road medians, and drainage paths without any containment.
